---
title: "TRUE Final Project"
author: "Jessica Aimunmondion, Joel Barnes, Zeek Moore"
date: "2025-04-29"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

#collecting the Data 

```{r}
#Retrieving the static house data frame and meta data frame
library(arrow)
library(tidyverse)
houseDF = read_parquet('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/static_house_info.parquet')
metaDF = read_csv('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/data_dictionary.csv')
#Filter out the houses that only use electricity
library(data.table)
houseDF = houseDF[c(houseDF$in.clothes_dryer %like% 'Electric', houseDF$in.clothes_washer %like% 'Electric' ),]
#Finding out all the different county codes and collect the top most populated counties
county =data.frame(head(sort(table(houseDF$in.county), decreasing = TRUE), 10))
#Separate each house base off county code
c1 = houseDF[houseDF$in.county == county[1,1],]
c2 = houseDF[houseDF$in.county == county[2,1],]
c3 = houseDF[houseDF$in.county == county[3,1],]
c4 = houseDF[houseDF$in.county == county[4,1],]
c5 = houseDF[houseDF$in.county == county[5,1],]
#c6 = houseDF[houseDF$in.county == county[6,1],]
#c7 = houseDF[houseDF$in.county == county[7,1],]
#c8 = houseDF[houseDF$in.county == county[8,1],]
#c9 = houseDF[houseDF$in.county == county[9,1],]
#c10 = houseDF[houseDF$in.county == county[10,1],]
```

```{r}
#Sampling ten houses for each data set 
houses1 = sample(c1$bldg_id, size = 10, replace = TRUE)
houses2 = sample(c2$bldg_id, size = 10, replace = TRUE)
houses3 = sample(c3$bldg_id, size = 10, replace = TRUE)
houses4 = sample(c4$bldg_id, size = 10, replace = TRUE)
houses5 = sample(c5$bldg_id, size = 10, replace = TRUE)
#houses6 = sample(c6$bldg_id, size = 10, replace = TRUE)
#houses7 = sample(c7$bldg_id, size = 10, replace = TRUE)
#houses8 = sample(c8$bldg_id, size = 10, replace = TRUE)
#houses9 = sample(c9$bldg_id, size = 10, replace = TRUE)
#houses10 = sample(c10$bldg_id, size = 10, replace = TRUE)
```

#Collecting and filtering the the county weather 

```{r}
#Collecting the weather data from the top ten populated counties
county$paste = paste0(trimws(county$Var1),'.csv' )
county$paste = trimws(county$paste)
weathercd1 = read_csv(trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[1,3])))
weathercd2 = read_csv(trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[2,3])))
weathercd3 = read_csv(trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[3,3])))
weathercd4 = read_csv(trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[4,3])))
weathercd5 = read_csv(trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[5,3])))
#weathercd6 = read_csv(trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[6,3])))
#weathercd7 = read_csv(trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[7,3])))
#weathercd8 = read_csv(trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[8,3])))
#weathercd9 = read_csv(trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[9,3])))
#weathercd10 = read_csv(trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[10,3])))
#trimws(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/weather/2023-weather-data/',county[1,3]))
```

```{r}
#Filtering out each weather date frame to only contain the month of July and only collect the first four columns which are the temperature in Celsius, humidity , and wind speed 
weathercd1 = weathercd1[weathercd1$date_time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & weathercd1$date_time < '2018-07-31 20:00:00',c(1:4)]
weathercd2 = weathercd2[weathercd2$date_time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & weathercd2$date_time < '2018-07-31 20:00:00',c(1:4)]
weathercd3 = weathercd3[weathercd3$date_time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & weathercd3$date_time < '2018-07-31 20:00:00',c(1:4)]
weathercd4 = weathercd4[weathercd4$date_time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & weathercd4$date_time < '2018-07-31 20:00:00',c(1:4)]
weathercd5 = weathercd5[weathercd5$date_time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & weathercd5$date_time < '2018-07-31 20:00:00',c(1:4)]
#weathercd6 = weathercd6[weathercd6$date_time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & weathercd6$date_time < '2018-07-31 20:00:00',c(1:4)]
#weathercd7 = weathercd7[weathercd7$date_time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & weathercd7$date_time < '2018-07-31 20:00:00',c(1:4)]
#weathercd8 = weathercd8[weathercd8$date_time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & weathercd8$date_time < '2018-07-31 20:00:00',c(1:4)]
#weathercd9 = weathercd9[weathercd9$date_time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & weathercd9$date_time < '2018-07-31 20:00:00',c(1:4)]
#weathercd10 = weathercd10[weathercd10$date_time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & weathercd10$date_time < '2018-07-31 20:00:00',c(1:4)]
```

```{r}
#Add a new column to convert the temperature to Fahrenheit by creating function to convert it from Celsius
convert_temp_to_fahrenheit <- function(celsius_temp) {
  farenheit_temp <- celsius_temp * 9/5 + 32
  return(farenheit_temp)
}
weathercd1$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(weathercd1$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
weathercd2$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(weathercd2$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
weathercd3$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(weathercd3$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
weathercd4$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(weathercd4$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
weathercd5$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(weathercd5$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
#weathercd6$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(weathercd6$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
#weathercd7$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(weathercd7$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
#weathercd8$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(weathercd8$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
#weathercd9$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(weathercd9$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
#weathercd10$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(weathercd10$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
```


#Collecting the sample energy data


```{r}
#Collecting the energy information from the house building based of the county (starting with the most populated county)
#Naming convention: enen= energy of the n house; cdn = county code of the house 
ene1cd1 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses1[1]),'.parquet'))
ene2cd1 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses1[2]),'.parquet'))
ene3cd1 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses1[3]),'.parquet'))
ene4cd1 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses1[4]),'.parquet'))
ene5cd1 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses1[5]),'.parquet'))
ene6cd1 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses1[6]),'.parquet'))
ene7cd1 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses1[7]),'.parquet'))
ene8cd1 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses1[8]),'.parquet'))
ene9cd1 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses1[9]),'.parquet'))
ene10cd1 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses1[10]),'.parquet'))
```

```{r}
#County 2
ene1cd2 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses2[1]),'.parquet'))
ene2cd2 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses2[2]),'.parquet'))
ene3cd2 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses2[3]),'.parquet'))
ene4cd2 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses2[4]),'.parquet'))
ene5cd2 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses2[5]),'.parquet'))
ene6cd2 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses2[6]),'.parquet'))
ene7cd2 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses2[7]),'.parquet'))
ene8cd2 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses2[8]),'.parquet'))
ene9cd2 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses2[9]),'.parquet'))
ene10cd2 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses2[10]),'.parquet'))
```

```{r}
#County 3
ene1cd3 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses3[1]),'.parquet'))
ene2cd3 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses3[2]),'.parquet'))
ene3cd3 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses3[3]),'.parquet'))
ene4cd3 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses3[4]),'.parquet'))
ene5cd3 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses3[5]),'.parquet'))
ene6cd3 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses3[6]),'.parquet'))
ene7cd3 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses3[7]),'.parquet'))
ene8cd3 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses3[8]),'.parquet'))
ene9cd3 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses3[9]),'.parquet'))
ene10cd3 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses3[10]),'.parquet'))
```

```{r}
#County 4
ene1cd4 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses4[1]),'.parquet'))
ene2cd4 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses4[2]),'.parquet'))
ene3cd4 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses4[3]),'.parquet'))
ene4cd4 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses4[4]),'.parquet'))
ene5cd4 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses4[5]),'.parquet'))
ene6cd4 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses4[6]),'.parquet'))
ene7cd4 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses4[7]),'.parquet'))
ene8cd4 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses4[8]),'.parquet'))
ene9cd4 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses4[9]),'.parquet'))
ene10cd4 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses4[10]),'.parquet'))
```

```{r}
#County 5
ene1cd5 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses5[1]),'.parquet'))
ene2cd5 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses5[2]),'.parquet'))
ene3cd5 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses5[3]),'.parquet'))
ene4cd5 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses5[4]),'.parquet'))
ene5cd5 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses5[5]),'.parquet'))
ene6cd5 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses5[6]),'.parquet'))
ene7cd5 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses5[7]),'.parquet'))
ene8cd5 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses5[8]),'.parquet'))
ene9cd5 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses5[9]),'.parquet'))
ene10cd5 = read_parquet(paste0('https://intro-datascience.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/SC-data/2023-houseData/',as.character(houses5[10]),'.parquet'))
```

#Filltering out the July Data

```{r}
#Filtering the data to only contain the month of July and only uses electrical energy 
library(data.table)
ene1cd1 = ene1cd1[ene1cd1$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene1cd1$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene1cd1) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene1cd1) == 'time')]
ene2cd1 = ene2cd1[ene2cd1$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene2cd1$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene2cd1) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene2cd1) == 'time')]
ene3cd1 = ene3cd1[ene3cd1$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene3cd1$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene3cd1) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene3cd1) == 'time')]
ene4cd1 = ene4cd1[ene4cd1$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene4cd1$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene4cd1) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene4cd1) == 'time')]
ene5cd1 = ene5cd1[ene5cd1$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene5cd1$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene5cd1) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene5cd1) == 'time')]
ene6cd1 = ene6cd1[ene6cd1$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene6cd1$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene6cd1) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene6cd1) == 'time')]
ene7cd1 = ene7cd1[ene7cd1$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene7cd1$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene7cd1) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene7cd1) == 'time')]
ene8cd1 = ene8cd1[ene8cd1$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene8cd1$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene8cd1) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene8cd1) == 'time')]
ene9cd1 = ene9cd1[ene9cd1$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene9cd1$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene9cd1) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene9cd1) == 'time')]
ene10cd1 = ene10cd1[ene10cd1$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene10cd1$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene10cd1) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene10cd1) == 'time')]
```

```{r}
#County 2
ene1cd2 = ene1cd2[ene1cd2$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene1cd2$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene1cd2) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene1cd2) == 'time')]
ene2cd2 = ene2cd2[ene2cd2$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene2cd2$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene2cd2) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene2cd2) == 'time')]
ene3cd2 = ene3cd2[ene3cd2$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene3cd2$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene3cd2) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene3cd2) == 'time')]
ene4cd2 = ene4cd2[ene4cd2$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene4cd2$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene4cd2) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene4cd2) == 'time')]
ene5cd2 = ene5cd2[ene5cd2$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene5cd2$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene5cd2) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene5cd2) == 'time')]
ene6cd2 = ene6cd2[ene6cd2$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene6cd2$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene6cd2) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene6cd2) == 'time')]
ene7cd2 = ene7cd2[ene7cd2$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene7cd2$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene7cd2) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene7cd2) == 'time')]
ene8cd2 = ene8cd2[ene8cd2$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene8cd2$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene8cd2) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene8cd2) == 'time')]
ene9cd2 = ene9cd2[ene9cd2$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene9cd2$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene9cd2) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene9cd2) == 'time')]
ene10cd2 = ene10cd2[ene10cd2$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene10cd2$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene10cd2) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene10cd2) == 'time')]
```

```{r}
#County 3
ene1cd3 = ene1cd3[ene1cd3$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene1cd3$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene1cd3) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene1cd3) == 'time')]
ene2cd3 = ene2cd3[ene2cd3$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene2cd3$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene2cd3) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene2cd3) == 'time')]
ene3cd3 = ene3cd3[ene3cd3$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene3cd3$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene3cd3) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene3cd3) == 'time')]
ene4cd3 = ene4cd3[ene4cd3$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene4cd3$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene4cd3) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene4cd3) == 'time')]
ene5cd3 = ene5cd3[ene5cd3$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene5cd3$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene5cd3) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene5cd3) == 'time')]
ene6cd3 = ene6cd3[ene6cd3$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene6cd3$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene6cd3) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene6cd3) == 'time')]
ene7cd3 = ene7cd3[ene7cd3$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene7cd3$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene7cd3) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene7cd3) == 'time')]
ene8cd3 = ene8cd3[ene8cd3$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene8cd3$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene8cd3) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene8cd3) == 'time')]
ene9cd3 = ene9cd3[ene9cd3$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene9cd3$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene9cd3) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene9cd3) == 'time')]
ene10cd3 = ene10cd3[ene10cd3$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene10cd3$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene10cd3) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene10cd3) == 'time')]
```

```{r}
#County 4
ene1cd4 = ene1cd4[ene1cd4$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene1cd4$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene1cd4) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene1cd4) == 'time')]
ene2cd4 = ene2cd4[ene2cd4$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene2cd4$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene2cd4) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene2cd4) == 'time')]
ene3cd4 = ene3cd4[ene3cd4$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene3cd4$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene3cd4) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene3cd4) == 'time')]
ene4cd4 = ene4cd4[ene4cd4$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene4cd4$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene4cd4) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene4cd4) == 'time')]
ene5cd4 = ene5cd4[ene5cd4$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene5cd4$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene5cd4) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene5cd4) == 'time')]
ene6cd4 = ene6cd4[ene6cd4$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene6cd4$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene6cd4) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene6cd4) == 'time')]
ene7cd4 = ene7cd4[ene7cd4$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene7cd4$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene7cd4) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene7cd4) == 'time')]
ene8cd4 = ene8cd4[ene8cd4$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene8cd4$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene8cd4) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene8cd4) == 'time')]
ene9cd4 = ene9cd4[ene9cd4$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene9cd4$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene9cd4) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene9cd4) == 'time')]
ene10cd4 = ene10cd4[ene10cd4$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene10cd4$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene10cd4) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene10cd4) == 'time')]
```

```{r}
#County 5
ene1cd5 = ene1cd5[ene1cd5$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene1cd5$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene1cd5) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene1cd5) == 'time')]
ene2cd5 = ene2cd5[ene2cd5$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene2cd5$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene2cd5) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene2cd5) == 'time')]
ene3cd5 = ene3cd5[ene3cd5$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene3cd5$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene3cd5) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene3cd5) == 'time')]
ene4cd5 = ene4cd5[ene4cd5$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene4cd5$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene4cd5) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene4cd5) == 'time')]
ene5cd5 = ene5cd5[ene5cd5$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene5cd5$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene5cd5) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene5cd5) == 'time')]
ene6cd5 = ene6cd5[ene6cd5$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene6cd5$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene6cd5) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene6cd5) == 'time')]
ene7cd5 = ene7cd5[ene7cd5$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene7cd5$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene7cd5) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene7cd5) == 'time')]
ene8cd5 = ene8cd5[ene8cd5$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene8cd5$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene8cd5) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene8cd5) == 'time')]
ene9cd5 = ene9cd5[ene9cd5$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene9cd5$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene9cd5) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene9cd5) == 'time')]
ene10cd5 = ene10cd5[ene10cd5$time >= '2018-06-30 20:00:00' & ene10cd5$time <= '2018-08-01 00:00:00', (colnames(ene10cd5) %like% 'out.electricity' | colnames(ene10cd5) == 'time')]
```

#Calculating the total electricity usage

```{r}
#Creating a new column to record the total sum of hourly data
ene1cd1$total.electricity = rowSums(ene1cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene2cd1$total.electricity = rowSums(ene2cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene3cd1$total.electricity = rowSums(ene3cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene4cd1$total.electricity = rowSums(ene4cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene5cd1$total.electricity = rowSums(ene5cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene6cd1$total.electricity = rowSums(ene6cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene7cd1$total.electricity = rowSums(ene7cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene8cd1$total.electricity = rowSums(ene8cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene9cd1$total.electricity = rowSums(ene9cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene10cd1$total.electricity = rowSums(ene10cd1[,c(1:24)])
```

```{r}
#county 2
ene1cd2$total.electricity = rowSums(ene1cd2[,c(1:24)])
ene2cd2$total.electricity = rowSums(ene2cd2[,c(1:24)])
ene3cd2$total.electricity = rowSums(ene3cd2[,c(1:24)])
ene4cd2$total.electricity = rowSums(ene4cd2[,c(1:24)])
ene5cd2$total.electricity = rowSums(ene5cd2[,c(1:24)])
ene6cd2$total.electricity = rowSums(ene6cd2[,c(1:24)])
ene7cd2$total.electricity = rowSums(ene7cd2[,c(1:24)])
ene8cd2$total.electricity = rowSums(ene8cd2[,c(1:24)])
ene9cd2$total.electricity = rowSums(ene9cd2[,c(1:24)])
ene10cd2$total.electricity = rowSums(ene10cd2[,c(1:24)])
```

```{r}
#County 3
ene1cd3$total.electricity = rowSums(ene1cd3[,c(1:24)])
ene2cd3$total.electricity = rowSums(ene2cd3[,c(1:24)])
ene3cd3$total.electricity = rowSums(ene3cd3[,c(1:24)])
ene4cd3$total.electricity = rowSums(ene4cd3[,c(1:24)])
ene5cd3$total.electricity = rowSums(ene5cd3[,c(1:24)])
ene6cd3$total.electricity = rowSums(ene6cd3[,c(1:24)])
ene7cd3$total.electricity = rowSums(ene7cd3[,c(1:24)])
ene8cd3$total.electricity = rowSums(ene3cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene9cd3$total.electricity = rowSums(ene3cd1[,c(1:24)])
ene10cd3$total.electricity = rowSums(ene10cd3[,c(1:24)])
```

```{r}
#County 4
ene1cd4$total.electricity = rowSums(ene1cd4[,c(1:24)])
ene2cd4$total.electricity = rowSums(ene2cd4[,c(1:24)])
ene3cd4$total.electricity = rowSums(ene3cd4[,c(1:24)])
ene4cd4$total.electricity = rowSums(ene4cd4[,c(1:24)])
ene5cd4$total.electricity = rowSums(ene5cd4[,c(1:24)])
ene6cd4$total.electricity = rowSums(ene6cd4[,c(1:24)])
ene7cd4$total.electricity = rowSums(ene7cd4[,c(1:24)])
ene8cd4$total.electricity = rowSums(ene8cd4[,c(1:24)])
ene9cd4$total.electricity = rowSums(ene9cd4[,c(1:24)])
ene10cd4$total.electricity = rowSums(ene10cd4[,c(1:24)])
```

```{r}
#County 5
ene1cd5$total.electricity = rowSums(ene1cd5[,c(1:24)])
ene2cd5$total.electricity = rowSums(ene2cd5[,c(1:24)])
ene3cd5$total.electricity = rowSums(ene3cd5[,c(1:24)])
ene4cd5$total.electricity = rowSums(ene4cd5[,c(1:24)])
ene5cd5$total.electricity = rowSums(ene5cd5[,c(1:24)])
ene6cd5$total.electricity = rowSums(ene6cd5[,c(1:24)])
ene7cd5$total.electricity = rowSums(ene7cd5[,c(1:24)])
ene8cd5$total.electricity = rowSums(ene8cd5[,c(1:24)])
ene9cd5$total.electricity = rowSums(ene9cd5[,c(1:24)])
ene10cd5$total.electricity = rowSums(ene10cd5[,c(1:24)])
```

#Creating the total energy usage sample data frame

```{r}
#Combining all the total electricity columns into one dataframe
eneTotalcd1 = data.frame('house1'= ene1cd1$total.electricity,'house2'= ene2cd1$total.electricity,'house3'= ene3cd1$total.electricity,'house4'= ene4cd1$total.electricity,'house5'= ene5cd1$total.electricity,'house6'= ene6cd1$total.electricity,'house7'= ene7cd1$total.electricity,'house8'= ene8cd1$total.electricity,'house9'= ene9cd1$total.electricity,'house10'= ene10cd1$total.electricity,'time' = ene10cd1$time)
eneTotalcd1$date_time = as_datetime(eneTotalcd1$time)
eneTotalcd1$total = rowSums(eneTotalcd1[,c(1:10)])
eneTotalcd1$average = rowMeans(eneTotalcd1[,c(1:10)])
```

```{r}
#County 2
eneTotalcd2 = data.frame('house1'= ene1cd2$total.electricity,'house2'= ene2cd2$total.electricity,'house3'= ene3cd2$total.electricity,'house4'= ene4cd2$total.electricity,'house5'= ene5cd2$total.electricity,'house6'= ene6cd2$total.electricity,'house7'= ene7cd2$total.electricity,'house8'= ene8cd2$total.electricity,'house9'= ene9cd2$total.electricity,'house10'= ene10cd2$total.electricity,'time' = ene10cd2$time)
eneTotalcd2$date_time = as_datetime(eneTotalcd2$time)
eneTotalcd2$total = rowSums(eneTotalcd2[,c(1:10)])
eneTotalcd2$average = rowMeans(eneTotalcd2[,c(1:10)])
```

```{r}
#County 3
eneTotalcd3 = data.frame('house1'= ene1cd3$total.electricity,'house2'= ene2cd3$total.electricity,'house3'= ene3cd3$total.electricity,'house4'= ene4cd3$total.electricity,'house5'= ene5cd3$total.electricity,'house6'= ene6cd3$total.electricity,'house7'= ene7cd3$total.electricity,'house8'= ene8cd1$total.electricity,'house9'= ene9cd3$total.electricity,'house10'= ene10cd3$total.electricity,'time' = ene10cd3$time)
eneTotalcd3$date_time = as_datetime(eneTotalcd3$time)
eneTotalcd3$total = rowSums(eneTotalcd3[,c(1:10)])
eneTotalcd3$average = rowMeans(eneTotalcd3[,c(1:10)])
```

```{r}
#County 4
eneTotalcd4 = data.frame('house1'= ene1cd4$total.electricity,'house2'= ene2cd4$total.electricity,'house3'= ene3cd4$total.electricity,'house4'= ene4cd4$total.electricity,'house5'= ene5cd4$total.electricity,'house6'= ene6cd4$total.electricity,'house7'= ene7cd4$total.electricity,'house8'= ene8cd4$total.electricity,'house9'= ene9cd4$total.electricity,'house10'= ene10cd4$total.electricity,'time' = ene10cd4$time)
eneTotalcd4$date_time = as_datetime(eneTotalcd4$time)
eneTotalcd4$total = rowSums(eneTotalcd4[,c(1:10)])
eneTotalcd4$average = rowMeans(eneTotalcd4[,c(1:10)])
```

```{r}
#County 5
eneTotalcd5 = data.frame('house1'= ene1cd5$total.electricity,'house2'= ene2cd5$total.electricity,'house3'= ene3cd5$total.electricity,'house4'= ene4cd5$total.electricity,'house5'= ene5cd5$total.electricity,'house6'= ene6cd5$total.electricity,'house7'= ene7cd5$total.electricity,'house8'= ene8cd5$total.electricity,'house9'= ene9cd5$total.electricity,'house10'= ene10cd5$total.electricity,'time' = ene10cd5$time)
eneTotalcd5$date_time = as_datetime(eneTotalcd5$time)
eneTotalcd5$total = rowSums(eneTotalcd5[,c(1:10)])
eneTotalcd5$average = rowMeans(eneTotalcd5[,c(1:10)])
```

#Mearging the energy and weather data frames based on time

```{r}
#Merging the weather data and the energy data based on county code
library(dplyr)
WeaEneCd1 = eneTotalcd1 %>%
  left_join(weathercd1, by = "date_time")
#Getting rid of the extra time colum
WeaEneCd1 = WeaEneCd1[,c(1:11,13:18)]
#Creating a group column based on the hour
WeaEneCd1$hour = format(WeaEneCd1$time, "%H")
```

```{r}
#County 2
WeaEneCd2 = eneTotalcd2 %>%
  left_join(weathercd2, by = "date_time")
#Getting rid of the extra time colum
WeaEneCd2 = WeaEneCd2[,c(1:11,13:18)]
#Creating a group column based on the hour
WeaEneCd2$hour = format(WeaEneCd2$time, "%H")
```

```{r}
#County 3
WeaEneCd3 = eneTotalcd3 %>%
  left_join(weathercd3, by = "date_time")
#Getting rid of the extra time colum
WeaEneCd3 = WeaEneCd3[,c(1:11,13:18)]
#Creating a group column based on the hour
WeaEneCd3$hour = format(WeaEneCd3$time, "%H")
```

```{r}
#County 4
WeaEneCd4 = eneTotalcd4 %>%
  left_join(weathercd4, by = "date_time")
#Getting rid of the extra time colum
WeaEneCd4 = WeaEneCd4[,c(1:11,13:18)]
#Creating a group column based on the hour
WeaEneCd4$hour = format(WeaEneCd4$time, "%H")
```

```{r}
#County 5
WeaEneCd5 = eneTotalcd5 %>%
  left_join(weathercd5, by = "date_time")
#Getting rid of the extra time colum
WeaEneCd5 = WeaEneCd5[,c(1:11,13:18)]
#Creating a group column based on the hour
WeaEneCd5$hour = format(WeaEneCd5$time, "%H")
```

#Creating the hourly data frame for each county 

```{r}
#Creating a group data frame based on hourly data per county code
hourly_datacd1 = WeaEneCd1 %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarize(avg_electricity =  mean(total, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_temperature = mean(`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .group ="drop"
            )
```

```{r}
#County 2
hourly_datacd2 = WeaEneCd2 %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarize(avg_electricity =  mean(total, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_temperature = mean(`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .group ="drop"
            )
```

```{r}
#County 3
hourly_datacd3 = WeaEneCd3 %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarize(avg_electricity =  mean(total, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_temperature = mean(`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .group ="drop"
            )
```

```{r}
#County 4
hourly_datacd4 = WeaEneCd4 %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarize(avg_electricity =  mean(total, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_temperature = mean(`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .group ="drop"
            )
```

```{r}
#County 5 
hourly_datacd5 = WeaEneCd5 %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarize(avg_electricity =  mean(total, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_temperature = mean(`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .group ="drop"
            )
```

#Creating the Predictive Model 

```{r}
#Creating a new data frame that collects the total energy sum from each county 
modelcd1 = data.frame('total.energy' = WeaEneCd1$total, 'temperature'=  WeaEneCd1$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, 'wind.speed'= WeaEneCd1$`Wind Speed [m/s]`,'humidity'= WeaEneCd1$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
#Getting rid of any missing data 
modelcd1 = na.omit(modelcd1)
#Making the model
lm_total1 = lm(total.energy ~ ., data = modelcd1)
summary(lm_total1)
```

```{r}
#County 2
modelcd2 = data.frame('total.energy' = WeaEneCd2$total, 'temperature'=  WeaEneCd2$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, 'wind.speed'= WeaEneCd2$`Wind Speed [m/s]`,'humidity'= WeaEneCd2$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
#Getting rid of any missing data 
modelcd2 = na.omit(modelcd2)
#Making the model
lm_total2 = lm(total.energy ~ ., data = modelcd2)
summary(lm_total2)
```

```{r}
#County 3
modelcd3 = data.frame('total.energy' = WeaEneCd3$total, 'temperature'=  WeaEneCd3$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, 'wind.speed'= WeaEneCd3$`Wind Speed [m/s]`,'humidity'= WeaEneCd3$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
#Getting rid of any missing data 
modelcd3 = na.omit(modelcd3)
#Making the model
lm_total3 = lm(total.energy ~ ., data = modelcd3)
summary(lm_total3)
```

```{r}
#County 4
modelcd4 = data.frame('total.energy' = WeaEneCd4$total, 'temperature'=  WeaEneCd4$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, 'wind.speed'= WeaEneCd4$`Wind Speed [m/s]`,'humidity'= WeaEneCd4$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
#Getting rid of any missing data 
modelcd4 = na.omit(modelcd4)
#Making the model
lm_total4 = lm(total.energy ~ ., data = modelcd4)
summary(lm_total4)
```

```{r}
#County 5
modelcd5 = data.frame('total.energy' = WeaEneCd5$total, 'temperature'=  WeaEneCd5$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, 'wind.speed'= WeaEneCd5$`Wind Speed [m/s]`,'humidity'= WeaEneCd5$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
#Getting rid of any missing data 
modelcd5 = na.omit(modelcd5)
#Making the model
lm_total5 = lm(total.energy ~ ., data = modelcd5)
summary(lm_total5)
```

#Creating new July Weather

```{r}
#Adding 5 Celsius to the temperature to be our predictive model
NEWWeaEneCd1 = WeaEneCd1
NEWWeaEneCd1$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]` = WeaEneCd1$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]` + 5
NEWWeaEneCd1$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(NEWWeaEneCd1$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
```

```{r}
#County 2
NEWWeaEneCd2 = WeaEneCd2
NEWWeaEneCd2$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]` = WeaEneCd2$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]` + 5
NEWWeaEneCd2$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(NEWWeaEneCd2$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
```

```{r}
#County 3
NEWWeaEneCd3 = WeaEneCd3
NEWWeaEneCd3$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]` = WeaEneCd3$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]` + 5
NEWWeaEneCd3$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(NEWWeaEneCd3$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
```

```{r}
#County 4
NEWWeaEneCd4 = WeaEneCd4
NEWWeaEneCd4$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]` = WeaEneCd4$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]` + 5
NEWWeaEneCd4$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(NEWWeaEneCd4$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
```

```{r}
#County 5
NEWWeaEneCd5 = WeaEneCd5
NEWWeaEneCd5$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]` = WeaEneCd5$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]` + 5
NEWWeaEneCd5$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]` = convert_temp_to_fahrenheit(NEWWeaEneCd5$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°C]`)
```

## Creating energy functions based off the new weather

```{r}
#Creating a function based off the liner model
NEWWeaEneCd1$total = -6.09309 + (0.21030*NEWWeaEneCd1$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`) + -(0.29582*NEWWeaEneCd1$`Wind Speed [m/s]`) + (0.01537*NEWWeaEneCd1$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
NEWWeaEneCd1 = NEWWeaEneCd1[,c(11:18)]
```

```{r}
#County 2
NEWWeaEneCd2$total = 6.123680 + (0.050118*NEWWeaEneCd2$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`) + (0.304356*NEWWeaEneCd2$`Wind Speed [m/s]`) + (0.001636*NEWWeaEneCd2$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
NEWWeaEneCd2 = NEWWeaEneCd2[,c(11:18)]
```

```{r}
#County 3
NEWWeaEneCd3$total = -13.892912 + (0.327632*NEWWeaEneCd3$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`) + (-0.587691*NEWWeaEneCd3$`Wind Speed [m/s]`) + (-0.009852*NEWWeaEneCd3$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
NEWWeaEneCd3 = NEWWeaEneCd3[,c(11:18)]
```

```{r}
#County 4
NEWWeaEneCd4$total = -14.425045 + (0.294598*NEWWeaEneCd4$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`) + (0.097476*NEWWeaEneCd4$`Wind Speed [m/s]`) + (0.008676*NEWWeaEneCd4$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
NEWWeaEneCd4 = NEWWeaEneCd4[,c(11:18)]
```

```{r}
#County 5
NEWWeaEneCd5$total = -7.93948 + (0.25077*NEWWeaEneCd5$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`) + (-0.23788*NEWWeaEneCd5$`Wind Speed [m/s]`) + (0.01002*NEWWeaEneCd5$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
NEWWeaEneCd5 = NEWWeaEneCd5[,c(11:18)]
```

#New Hourly Data Collection
```{r}
#Grouping the hourly usage of electricity and temperature group by hour 
NEWhourly_datacd1 = NEWWeaEneCd1 %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarize(avg_electricity =  mean(total, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_temperature = mean(`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .group ="drop"
            )
```

```{r}
#County 2
NEWhourly_datacd2 = NEWWeaEneCd2 %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarize(avg_electricity =  mean(total, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_temperature = mean(`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .group ="drop"
            )
```

```{r}
#County 3
NEWhourly_datacd3 = NEWWeaEneCd3 %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarize(avg_electricity =  mean(total, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_temperature = mean(`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .group ="drop"
            )
```

```{r}
#County 4
NEWhourly_datacd4 = NEWWeaEneCd4 %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarize(avg_electricity =  mean(total, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_temperature = mean(`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .group ="drop"
            )
```

```{r}
#County 5
NEWhourly_datacd5 = NEWWeaEneCd5 %>%
  group_by(hour) %>%
  summarize(avg_electricity =  mean(total, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_temperature = mean(`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, na.rm = TRUE),
            .group ="drop"
            )
```

#Predictive Modeling

```{r}
library(caret)

# Set up 10-fold cross-validation
train_control1 <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10)

# Fit the model with cross-validation
cv_model1 <- train(total.energy ~ 
                    temperature + 
                    humidity + 
                    wind.speed, 
                  data = modelcd1, 
                  method = "lm", 
                  trControl = train_control1)

# View the cross-validation results
print(cv_model1)

svm_model1 <- svm(total.energy ~ .,
                 data = modelcd1,
                 type = "eps-regression",  # Regression
                 kernel = "radial")        # Radial kernel (non-linear)
NEWpredict1 = data.frame('total' = NEWWeaEneCd1$total, 'temperature' = NEWWeaEneCd1$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, 'wind.speed' = NEWWeaEneCd1$`Wind Speed [m/s]`, 'humidity' = NEWWeaEneCd1$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
NEWpredict1 = na.omit(NEWpredict1)
svm_predict1 = predict(svm_model1, newdata = NEWpredict1 )
saveRDS(svm_model1, "svm_model_cd1.rds")
```

```{r}
#County 2
# Set up 10-fold cross-validation
train_control2 <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10)

# Fit the model with cross-validation
cv_model2 <- train(total.energy ~ 
                    temperature + 
                    humidity + 
                    wind.speed, 
                  data = modelcd2, 
                  method = "lm", 
                  trControl = train_control2)

# View the cross-validation results
print(cv_model2)

svm_model2 <- svm(total.energy ~ .,
                 data = modelcd2,
                 type = "eps-regression",  # Regression
                 kernel = "radial")        # Radial kernel (non-linear)
NEWpredict2 = data.frame('total' = NEWWeaEneCd2$total, 'temperature' = NEWWeaEneCd2$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, 'wind.speed' = NEWWeaEneCd2$`Wind Speed [m/s]`, 'humidity' = NEWWeaEneCd2$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
NEWpredict2 = na.omit(NEWpredict2)
svm_predict2 = predict(svm_model2, newdata = NEWpredict2 )
saveRDS(svm_model2, "svm_model_cd2.rds")
```

```{r}
#County 3
# Set up 10-fold cross-validation
train_control3 <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10)

# Fit the model with cross-validation
cv_model3 <- train(total.energy ~ 
                    temperature + 
                    humidity + 
                    wind.speed, 
                  data = modelcd3, 
                  method = "lm", 
                  trControl = train_control3)

# View the cross-validation results
print(cv_model3)

svm_model3 <- svm(total.energy ~ .,
                 data = modelcd3,
                 type = "eps-regression",  # Regression
                 kernel = "radial")        # Radial kernel (non-linear)
NEWpredict3 = data.frame('total' = NEWWeaEneCd3$total, 'temperature' = NEWWeaEneCd3$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, 'wind.speed' = NEWWeaEneCd3$`Wind Speed [m/s]`, 'humidity' = NEWWeaEneCd3$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
NEWpredict3 = na.omit(NEWpredict3)
svm_predict3 = predict(svm_model3, newdata = NEWpredict3 )
saveRDS(svm_model3, "svm_model_cd3.rds")
```

```{r}
#County 4
# Set up 10-fold cross-validation
train_control4<- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10)

# Fit the model with cross-validation
cv_model4 <- train(total.energy ~ 
                    temperature + 
                    humidity + 
                    wind.speed, 
                  data = modelcd4, 
                  method = "lm", 
                  trControl = train_control4)

# View the cross-validation results
print(cv_model4)

svm_model4 <- svm(total.energy ~ .,
                 data = modelcd4,
                 type = "eps-regression",  # Regression
                 kernel = "radial")        # Radial kernel (non-linear)
NEWpredict4 = data.frame('total' = NEWWeaEneCd4$total, 'temperature' = NEWWeaEneCd4$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, 'wind.speed' = NEWWeaEneCd4$`Wind Speed [m/s]`, 'humidity' = NEWWeaEneCd4$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
NEWpredict4 = na.omit(NEWpredict4)
svm_predict4 = predict(svm_model4, newdata = NEWpredict4 )
saveRDS(svm_model4, "svm_model_cd4.rds")
```

```{r}
#County 5
# Set up 10-fold cross-validation
train_control5 <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10)

# Fit the model with cross-validation
cv_model5 <- train(total.energy ~ 
                    temperature + 
                    humidity + 
                    wind.speed, 
                  data = modelcd5, 
                  method = "lm", 
                  trControl = train_control5)

# View the cross-validation results
print(cv_model5)

svm_model5 <- svm(total.energy ~ .,
                 data = modelcd5,
                 type = "eps-regression",  # Regression
                 kernel = "radial")        # Radial kernel (non-linear)
NEWpredict5 = data.frame('total' = NEWWeaEneCd5$total, 'temperature' = NEWWeaEneCd5$`Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, 'wind.speed' = NEWWeaEneCd5$`Wind Speed [m/s]`, 'humidity' = NEWWeaEneCd5$`Relative Humidity [%]`)
NEWpredict5 = na.omit(NEWpredict5)
svm_predict5 = predict(svm_model5, newdata = NEWpredict5 )
saveRDS(svm_model5, "svm_model_cd5.rds")
```

## Including Plots

#Ploting the Data

```{r}
#Plotting the weather and energy usage throughout the month
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(WeaEneCd1, aes(x = hour, y = `Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, group = hour)) + geom_boxplot(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1) + labs( x = "Time (July, in hours)", y = "Dry Bulb Temperature (°F)", title = "Dry Bulb Temperature Over Time (in County 1)") + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme( plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"))

ggplot(hourly_datacd1, aes(x = as.numeric(hour), y = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E63946", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E63946", size = 2) +
  xlab("Hour of the Day") +
  ylab("Average Electricity Consumption (kWh)") +
  ggtitle("Average Electricity Consumption by Hour (in County 1)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )

ggplot(hourly_datacd1, aes(x = hour, y = avg_temperature, color = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Avg Electricity Use with Temperature(in County 1)",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Avg Temperature Use (F)",
    color = "Electricity Use (kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#32CD32" , high = "#FF6347") +  # Bright red to bright green
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```


```{r}
#County 2
ggplot(WeaEneCd2, aes(x = hour, y = `Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, group = hour)) + geom_boxplot(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1) + labs( x = "Time (July, in hours)", y = "Dry Bulb Temperature (°F)", title = "Dry Bulb Temperature Over Time (in County 2)") + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme( plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"))

ggplot(hourly_datacd2, aes(x = as.numeric(hour), y = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E63946", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E63946", size = 2) +
  xlab("Hour of the Day") +
  ylab("Average Electricity Consumption (kWh)") +
  ggtitle("Average Electricity Consumption by Hour(in County 2)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
ggplot(hourly_datacd2, aes(x = hour, y = avg_temperature, color = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Avg Electricity Use with Temperature(in County 2)",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Avg Temperature Use (F)",
    color = "Electricity Use (kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#32CD32" , high = "#FF6347") +  # Bright red to bright green
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
#County 3
ggplot(WeaEneCd3, aes(x = hour, y = `Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, group = hour)) + geom_boxplot(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1) + labs( x = "Time (July, in hours)", y = "Dry Bulb Temperature (°F)", title = "Dry Bulb Temperature Over Time (in County 3)") + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme( plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"))

ggplot(hourly_datacd3, aes(x = as.numeric(hour), y = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E63946", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E63946", size = 2) +
  xlab("Hour of the Day") +
  ylab("Average Electricity Consumption (kWh)") +
  ggtitle("Average Electricity Consumption by Hour(in County 3)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
ggplot(hourly_datacd3, aes(x = hour, y = avg_temperature, color = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Avg Electricity Use with Temperature(in County 3)",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Avg Temperature Use (F)",
    color = "Electricity Use (kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#32CD32" , high = "#FF6347") +  # Bright red to bright green
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
#County 4
ggplot(WeaEneCd4, aes(x = hour, y = `Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, group = hour)) + geom_boxplot(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1) + labs( x = "Time (July, in hours)", y = "Dry Bulb Temperature (°F)", title = "Dry Bulb Temperature Over Time (in County 4)") + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme( plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"))

ggplot(hourly_datacd4, aes(x = as.numeric(hour), y = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E63946", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E63946", size = 2) +
  xlab("Hour of the Day") +
  ylab("Average Electricity Consumption (kWh)") +
  ggtitle("Average Electricity Consumption by Hour(in County 4)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
ggplot(hourly_datacd4, aes(x = hour, y = avg_temperature, color = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Avg Electricity Use with Temperature(in County 4)",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Avg Temperature Use (F)",
    color = "Electricity Use (kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#32CD32" , high = "#FF6347") +  # Bright red to bright green
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
#County 5
ggplot(WeaEneCd5, aes(x = hour, y = `Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, group = hour)) + geom_boxplot(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1) + labs( x = "Time (July, in hours)", y = "Dry Bulb Temperature (°F)", title = "Dry Bulb Temperature Over Time (in County 5)") + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme( plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"))

ggplot(hourly_datacd5, aes(x = as.numeric(hour), y = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E63946", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E63946", size = 2) +
  xlab("Hour of the Day") +
  ylab("Average Electricity Consumption (kWh)") +
  ggtitle("Average Electricity Consumption by Hour (in County 5)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
ggplot(hourly_datacd5, aes(x = hour, y = avg_temperature, color = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Avg Electricity Use with Temperature(in County 5)",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Avg Temperature Use (F)",
    color = "Electricity Use (kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#32CD32" , high = "#FF6347") +  # Bright red to bright green
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
plot(lm_total$residuals)
```

#NEW July Weather Data (Temperature increased by 5 degrees Celcius)

```{r}
ggplot(NEWWeaEneCd1, aes(x = hour, y = `Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, group = hour)) + geom_boxplot(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1) + labs( x = "Time (July, in hours)", y = "Dry Bulb Temperature (°F)", title = "Dry Bulb Temperature Over Time (in County 1 w/ Inc)") + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme( plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"))

ggplot(NEWhourly_datacd1, aes(x = as.numeric(hour), y = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E63946", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E63946", size = 2) +
  xlab("Hour of the Day") +
  ylab("Average Electricity Consumption (kWh)") +
  ggtitle("Average Electricity Consumption by Hour (in County 1 w/ Inc.)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
ggplot(NEWhourly_datacd1, aes(x = hour, y = avg_temperature, color = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Avg Electricity Use with Temperature(in County 1 w/ Inc.)",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Avg Temperature Use (F)",
    color = "Electricity Use (kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#32CD32" , high = "#FF6347") +  # Bright red to bright green
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
#County 2
ggplot(NEWWeaEneCd2, aes(x = hour, y = `Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, group = hour)) + geom_boxplot(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1) + labs( x = "Time (July, in hours)", y = "Dry Bulb Temperature (°F)", title = "Dry Bulb Temperature Over Time (in County 2 w/ Inc.)") + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme( plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"))

ggplot(NEWhourly_datacd2, aes(x = as.numeric(hour), y = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E63946", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E63946", size = 2) +
  xlab("Hour of the Day") +
  ylab("Average Electricity Consumption (kWh)") +
  ggtitle("Average Electricity Consumption by Hour (in County 2 w/ Inc.)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
ggplot(NEWhourly_datacd2, aes(x = hour, y = avg_temperature, color = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Avg Electricity Use with Temperature(in County 2 w/ Inc.)",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Avg Temperature Use (F)",
    color = "Electricity Use (kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#32CD32" , high = "#FF6347") +  # Bright red to bright green
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
#County 3
ggplot(NEWWeaEneCd3, aes(x = hour, y = `Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, group = hour)) + geom_boxplot(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1) + labs( x = "Time (July, in hours)", y = "Dry Bulb Temperature (°F)", title = "Dry Bulb Temperature Over Time (in County 3 w/ Inc.)") + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme( plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"))

ggplot(NEWhourly_datacd3, aes(x = as.numeric(hour), y = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E63946", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E63946", size = 2) +
  xlab("Hour of the Day") +
  ylab("Average Electricity Consumption (kWh)") +
  ggtitle("Average Electricity Consumption by Hour (in County 3 w/ Inc.)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
ggplot(NEWhourly_datacd3, aes(x = hour, y = avg_temperature, color = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Avg Electricity Use with Temperature(in County 3 w/ Inc.)",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Avg Temperature Use (F)",
    color = "Electricity Use (kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#32CD32" , high = "#FF6347") +  # Bright red to bright green
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
#County 4
ggplot(NEWWeaEneCd4, aes(x = hour, y = `Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, group = hour)) + geom_boxplot(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1) + labs( x = "Time (July, in hours)", y = "Dry Bulb Temperature (°F)", title = "Dry Bulb Temperature Over Time (in County 4 w/ Inc.)") + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme( plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"))

ggplot(NEWhourly_datacd4, aes(x = as.numeric(hour), y = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E63946", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E63946", size = 2) +
  xlab("Hour of the Day") +
  ylab("Average Electricity Consumption (kWh)") +
  ggtitle("Average Electricity Consumption by Hour (in County 4 w/ Inc.)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
ggplot(NEWhourly_datacd4, aes(x = hour, y = avg_temperature, color = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Avg Electricity Use with Temperature(in County 4 w/ Inc.)",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Avg Temperature Use (F)",
    color = "Electricity Use (kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#32CD32" , high = "#FF6347") +  # Bright red to bright green
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

```{r}
#County 5
ggplot(NEWWeaEneCd5, aes(x = hour, y = `Dry Bulb Temperature [°F]`, group = hour)) + geom_boxplot(color = "#1f78b4", size = 1) + labs( x = "Time (July, in hours)", y = "Dry Bulb Temperature (°F)", title = "Dry Bulb Temperature Over Time (in County 5)") + theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme( plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"), axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"), axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"))

ggplot(NEWhourly_datacd5, aes(x = as.numeric(hour), y = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E63946", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "#E63946", size = 2) +
  xlab("Hour of the Day") +
  ylab("Average Electricity Consumption (kWh)") +
  ggtitle("Average Electricity Consumption by Hour (in County 5 w/ Inc.)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:23) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
ggplot(NEWhourly_datacd5, aes(x = hour, y = avg_temperature, color = avg_electricity)) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title = "Hourly Avg Electricity Use with Temperature(in County 5 w/ Inc.)",
    x = "Hour of the Day",
    y = "Avg Temperature Use (F)",
    color = "Electricity Use (kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#32CD32" , high = "#FF6347") +  # Bright red to bright green
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30"),
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
```

#Actually VS Predicted

```{r}
# Create a data frame for plotting
svm_results1 <- data.frame(
  Actual = NEWpredict1$total,
  Predicted = svm_predict1
)

# Plot actual vs predicted values
ggplot(svm_results1, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(color = "red", alpha = 0.6) +  # Scatter plot of actual vs predicted
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "green") +  # 45-degree line (perfect prediction)
  labs(
    title = "SVM Model: Actual vs Predicted Total Electricity Consumption (in County 1)",
    x = "Actual Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)",
    y = "Predicted Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )

```

```{r}
# Create a data frame for plotting
svm_results2 <- data.frame(
  Actual = NEWpredict2$total,
  Predicted = svm_predict2
)

# Plot actual vs predicted values
ggplot(svm_results2, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(color = "red", alpha = 0.6) +  # Scatter plot of actual vs predicted
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "green") +  # 45-degree line (perfect prediction)
  labs(
    title = "SVM Model: Actual vs Predicted Total Electricity Consumption (in County 2)",
    x = "Actual Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)",
    y = "Predicted Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
```

```{r}
# Create a data frame for plotting
svm_results3 <- data.frame(
  Actual = NEWpredict3$total,
  Predicted = svm_predict3
)

# Plot actual vs predicted values
ggplot(svm_results3, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(color = "red", alpha = 0.6) +  # Scatter plot of actual vs predicted
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "green") +  # 45-degree line (perfect prediction)
  labs(
    title = "SVM Model: Actual vs Predicted Total Electricity Consumption (in County 3)",
    x = "Actual Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)",
    y = "Predicted Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
```

```{r}
# Create a data frame for plotting
svm_results4 <- data.frame(
  Actual = NEWpredict4$total,
  Predicted = svm_predict4
)

# Plot actual vs predicted values
ggplot(svm_results4, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(color = "red", alpha = 0.6) +  # Scatter plot of actual vs predicted
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "green") +  # 45-degree line (perfect prediction)
  labs(
    title = "SVM Model: Actual vs Predicted Total Electricity Consumption (in County 4)",
    x = "Actual Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)",
    y = "Predicted Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
```

```{r}
# Create a data frame for plotting
svm_results5 <- data.frame(
  Actual = NEWpredict5$total,
  Predicted = svm_predict5
)

# Plot actual vs predicted values
ggplot(svm_results5, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_point(color = "red", alpha = 0.6) +  # Scatter plot of actual vs predicted
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "green") +  # 45-degree line (perfect prediction)
  labs(
    title = "SVM Model: Actual vs Predicted Total Electricity Consumption (in County 5)",
    x = "Actual Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)",
    y = "Predicted Total Electricity Consumption (kWh)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "gray30")
  )
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
